name: GlyNAC in older adults aging-hallmarks randomized trial
summary: A small randomized placebo-controlled trial of 16 weeks of GlyNAC in older adults, with a young-adult reference cohort and broad redox, mitochondrial, metabolic, inflammatory, endothelial, and physical-function endpoints.
population: Older adults aged 61 to 80 years, with a young-adult reference group

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already in the public evidence layer, so this is not an open no-result watch item.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for NAD and Redox Restoration because they can show whether the intervention changes biomarkers, function, mechanistic endpoints, and safety in Older adults aged 61 to 80 years, with a young-adult reference group. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
